strategy: Attack with Ice or Storm (+35%); he resists Fire 80%.

He sits behind a -90% Tower Shield and replaces it whenever damaged. Use Steal Ward or Pierce (manipulation that removes helpful wards) to strip it without triggering a replace, then hit the same round.
Do not Trap him: a trap makes him Cleanse Ward himself.

Exploit his Halberd cheat: if the wizard he Star-hits has a non-absorb ward up, the attack stops and he strips all wards from that wizard and himself. Hold a shield to make him blow off his own Tower Shield.
Clear the three minions to make the shield cheat stop, but note that once minions are down he strips his own wards and Star-hits every round, so burst him fast after.
Hitter funnels blades, a support Pierces or steals the shield, and the healer covers the 600 Star hits.
cheatNotes: He opens round 1 with a -90% Tower Shield on himself, so you must strip or pierce it before hitting.
Damaging him makes him immediately replace the Tower Shield, so chipping just refreshes his defense.
Manipulation spells that remove helpful wards, like Steal Ward or Pierce, take the shield without letting him replace it. That is the safe way to strip it.
This shield cheat stops once all his minions are defeated.
Trapping him makes him Cleanse Ward himself, so set up with blades, not traps on him.
Every second round he hits all wizards for 600 Star damage, so shield up on those rounds.
The hit order flips each time the Star cheat fires, alternating right-to-left and left-to-right.
Once his minions are gone, that Star attack comes every round.
If the wizard he Star-hits has a non-absorb ward up, the attack fizzles on everyone else and he strips all wards from that wizard and himself. Hold a shield to force him to drop his own Tower Shield.
After his minions die he also strips all wards off himself, opening him up, so burst him down right after.
